fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Massachusetts Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Massachusetts — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license massachusetts, dental assistant certification massachusetts, RDA massachusetts, DANB CDA massachusetts, dental assistant radiography massachusetts

board.name: Massachusetts Board of Registration in Dentistry

license_types.title: Licensed Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Create an Account and Complete Online Application
steps.description: Register for a new account on the MassLicensing portal and complete the Dental Assistant online application in its entirety.

steps.title: Mail Required CORI Acknowledgment Form (DA-1)
steps.description: Print and complete the DA-1 (Criminal Offender Record Information Acknowledgment Form) from the Board's website and mail it to the Board of Registration in Dentistry.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official transcript from an ADA-accredited dental assisting program to be sent directly to the Board of Registration in Dentistry from your educational institution.

steps.title: Verify DANB Certification
steps.description: Ensure your current Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) or Certified Orthodontic Assistant (COA) certificate from the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB) is verified directly to the Board.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload proof of current Basic Life Support (BLS) for Healthcare Providers CPR certification and your complete immunization records directly to your MassLicensing application.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application on the MassLicensing portal and promptly respond to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Board.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Massachusetts Board of Registration in Dentistry by license.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Massachusetts requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Massachusetts.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $60. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the Massachusetts Board of Registration in Dentistry by license.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Massachusetts
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in Massachusetts, you must submit your application and supporting documents online through the official MassLicensing portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the MassLicensing portal, select 'Register a New Account,' and follow the prompts to create your account. Once registered, log in to start your application.
